description: Close all Storybook coverage gaps in the browser extension side panel: install the a11y addon, expand under-covered story files with additional variants, add play functions for automated interaction testing to key interactive components, and add systematic dark/light side-by-side stories.

userStories.title: Install and configure @storybook/addon-a11y
userStories.description: Add the @storybook/addon-a11y package and register it in the Storybook config so the a11y panel appears in the toolbar for every story.
acceptanceCriteria: @storybook/addon-a11y is listed in platform/browser-extension/package.json devDependencies
The addons array in platform/browser-extension/.storybook/main.ts includes '@storybook/addon-a11y'
npm run storybook:build in platform/browser-extension succeeds with the addon configured

userStories.title: Expand FailedPluginCard stories with additional variants
userStories.description: Add stories for long error text (expand/collapse), npm specifier format, and short error (no expand button) to FailedPluginCard.stories.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: FailedPluginCard.stories.tsx has at least 4 exported stories
A story exists with an error string longer than 100 characters that triggers the Show more/Show less button
A story exists with an npm-style specifier (e.g. @opentabs-dev/plugin-slack)
A story exists with a short error (under 100 chars) that does NOT show the expand button
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/FailedPluginCard.stories.tsx (expand from 1 to 4+ stories)

Context:
The current story file has only 1 story (17 lines):
```tsx
const Default: Story = {
  args: { plugin: { specifier: '/Users/dev/plugins/broken', error: 'Missing dist/tools.json' } },
};
```

The component (FailedPluginCard.tsx, 30 lines) accepts `{ plugin: FailedPluginState }` where `FailedPluginState` has `specifier: string` and `error: string`. The component shows a Show more/Show less toggle when `plugin.error.length > 100`.

Changes:
Add these stories:
- `ShortError` — current Default behavior (error under 100 chars, no expand button)
- `LongError` — error string > 100 chars to trigger the expand/collapse toggle. Use a realistic multi-line error like a stack trace or module resolution error.
- `NpmSpecifier` — specifier like `@opentabs-dev/plugin-slack@1.2.3` instead of a local path
- `AllStates` — composite story showing all variants stacked vertically (follow the pattern from other stories like NpmPluginCard.stories.tsx)

Rename the current `Default` to `ShortError` for clarity.

Follow existing patterns:
- Use `args`-based stories (the component is purely props-driven)
- Keep the `w-80` decorator already on meta
- Export all stories as named exports alongside `export default meta`

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the FailedPluginCard.tsx component itself
- Keep the existing meta decorator (`w-80` wrapper)

userStories.title: Expand Footer stories with theme and port variants
userStories.description: Add stories showing the Footer in dark mode, with different port values pre-seeded in the chrome.storage mock, and with the theme toggle interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer.stories.tsx has at least 3 exported stories
A story exists that renders the Footer with dark mode active (document.documentElement has 'dark' class)
A story exists that demonstrates the theme toggle button

userStories.title: Expand Empty stories with additional compositions
userStories.description: Add stories for the Empty compound component showing minimal usage (title only), custom icon, no description, and custom styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty.stories.tsx has at least 4 exported stories
A story exists showing Empty with only a title (no icon, no description, no separator)
A story exists showing Empty with a custom icon (not the default Ghost)
A story exists showing a composite of all variants
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Empty.stories.tsx (expand from 1 to 4+ stories)

Context:
The current story file has only 1 story (25 lines) showing the full compound composition:
```tsx
const Default: Story = {
  render: () => (
    <Empty>
      <Empty.Content>
        <Empty.Icon className="h-12 w-12 text-muted-foreground" />
        <Empty.Title>Nothing Here</Empty.Title>
        <Empty.Separator />
        <Empty.Description>No items to display.</Empty.Description>
      </Empty.Content>
    </Empty>
  ),
};
```

The Empty component (56 lines) is a compound component with sub-components via `Object.assign`:
- `Empty` — wrapper div with rounded border, bg-card, shadow
- `Empty.Content` — flex column with gap-3
- `Empty.Icon` — defaults to `<Ghost>` from lucide-react if no children
- `Empty.Title` — renders as h3 via `Text` component
- `Empty.Separator` — colored bar (h-1 w-16 bg-primary)
- `Empty.Description` — paragraph with muted text

Changes:
Add these stories:
- `Default` — keep as-is (full composition)
- `TitleOnly` — just `<Empty><Empty.Content><Empty.Title>Nothing Here</Empty.Title></Empty.Content></Empty>`
- `CustomIcon` — pass a different lucide icon as children to `Empty.Icon` (e.g., `<Search>` or `<FolderOpen>` from lucide-react)
- `NoSeparator` — full composition but without `<Empty.Separator />`
- `AllVariants` — composite story showing all above variants stacked vertically (follow the pattern from Alert.stories.tsx `AllStatuses`)

Follow the existing pattern: all stories use `render` functions since the compound component requires JSX composition. Keep the `w-80` decorator on meta.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the Empty.tsx component itself
- Import additional icons from 'lucide-react' for the CustomIcon story

userStories.title: Add play functions to retro primitive stories (Switch, Select, Input, Dialog)

Select.stories.tsx Interactive story has a play function that opens the dropdown and selects an option

userStories.title: Add play functions to app-level interactive stories (ConfirmationDialog, PermissionSelect, ToolRow)
userStories.description: Add play functions to ConfirmationDialog, PermissionSelect, and ToolRow stories to test user interactions like approving/denying confirmations, changing permissions, and toggling tool states.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx SingleConfirmation story has a play function that clicks Approve and verifies onRespond was called
PermissionSelect.stories.tsx Interactive story has a play function that changes the permission value
ToolRow.stories.tsx Interactive story has a play function that changes the permission and verifies the UI updates

userStories.title: Add play functions to PluginCard and SearchResults stories

acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.stories.tsx WithMenu story has a play function that opens the context menu
PluginCard.stories.tsx Ready story has a play function that expands the accordion to reveal tools
SearchResults.stories.tsx Both story has a play function that verifies results are visible

userStories.title: Add dark/light side-by-side stories via ThemeGrid decorator
userStories.description: Create a shared ThemeGrid decorator and add side-by-side dark/light stories to representative components across retro primitives and app-level components.
acceptanceCriteria: A reusable ThemeGrid helper exists that renders a story in both light and dark themes side by side
At least 5 component story files have a DarkLight or ThemePair story showing the component in both themes
The side-by-side stories include at least one retro primitive (Button or Alert) and at least one app-level component (PluginCard or ToolRow)
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/storybook-helpers.tsx (NEW — shared ThemeGrid helper) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Button.stories.tsx (add ThemePair story) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Alert.stories.tsx (add ThemePair story) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.stories.tsx (add ThemePair story) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx (add ThemePair story) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx (add ThemePair story) Context: The Storybook preview.ts has a global theme toggle that sets `.dark` on ``. But this toggles ALL stories at once — there's no way to see light and dark side-by-side. The approach is to create a helper component that renders two isolated containers: one with light theme vars and one with dark theme vars. The theme system works via CSS variables on `.dark` class (set on `` by preview.ts decorator). To show both themes simultaneously, each container needs its own CSS variable scope. Looking at `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css`, the theme is defined as CSS custom properties on `:root` (light) and `.dark` (dark). Changes: 1. Create `storybook-helpers.tsx` with a `ThemeGrid` component: ```tsx import type { ReactNode } from 'react'; const ThemeGrid = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
Light
{children}
Dark
{children}
); export { ThemeGrid }; ``` Note: The `.dark` class on the wrapper div should scope the CSS variables. Verify by checking if the Tailwind dark mode is set to `class` strategy. Read `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css` to confirm how dark mode CSS variables are applied — if they're on `.dark` selector, the above approach works. If they're on `:root.dark` or `html.dark`, the inner wrapper won't inherit them and a different approach is needed (e.g., using iframes or data-theme attribute). If the `.dark` class approach doesn't work at the div level (because CSS variables are scoped to `html.dark`), use an alternative: render the content twice and toggle the global theme between renders, or use `@container` queries. The simplest working approach may be to set CSS variables inline on each container. 2. Add ThemePair stories to each file: ```tsx import { ThemeGrid } from '../storybook-helpers'; // or for retro: import { ThemeGrid } from '../../storybook-helpers'; const ThemePair: Story = { render: () => ( {/* Component with representative props */} ), }; ``` Constraints: - Do NOT modify any component source files — only story files and the new helper - The ThemeGrid approach depends on how CSS variables are scoped — read `styles.css` first to determine the correct implementation - If the `.dark` class only works on ``, consider duplicating the CSS variable values as inline styles on the dark container instead - Keep the ThemePair stories simple — show the component's most common/representative state, not all variants - The storybook-helpers.tsx file should NOT be picked up by the stories glob (`**/*.stories.tsx`) — verify it won't be
